type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Please note that these data are for the exploration (temporary) incinerator which will be decommissioned in the fall of 2017. Dioxins and furans emissions were calculated using stack testing results that were completed over 3 days in October 2016.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The quantity of substance assigned to Other-Non-Point category refers to emissions from blasting. They have been included in previous years but under a different category. However, please note that carbon monoxide emissions in 2020 are higher than the previous year because production has increased significantly. Explosives used in 2019 were of the order of 850 tonnes whereas 3,744 tonnes were used in 2020. Also, the emission factor used in the 2020 declaration has been changed to further align with Canadian calculation methods. Emission factors from the « Pits and quarries reporting guide» have now been integrated to calculation methods.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: HCL emissions come mostly from the incinerator stack. Emission factors from 2020 and 2019 are practically the same. However, a slight typo in the 2019 calculations has led to a slight underestimation

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: Following calculation review, a small typo has been discovered, changing 2020 annual release to 1,506,006. In addition, approximately 10x time more waste rock was generated in 2020 compared to 2019.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Lead emissions come mostly from the incinerator stack. 2020 release has increased because sampling lead concentration is higher.

substance.en: Nitrate ion in solution at pH >= 6.0
substance.fr: Nitrate (ion en sol. à un pH de >= 6.0)

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Most of the fugitive emissions from the site come from road dust and wind erosion from stockpiles. 2020 had considerably more travelled kilometers than in 2019 (approximately 9 times more). Also, the stockpile inventory has increase from 2019 to 2020 because 2020 is the first full complete year of operation. The total active stockpile area increased by an approximate factor of 3.

A misinterpretation of the area displaced per blast has lead to overestimation of the dust emissions from blasting in the previous version of the report.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site
